feedback.text: The Digital Fairness Act should include that digitally acquired goods remain in the buyer’s possession and that access cannot be terminated at any time by firms, whether digital or not, because the product has been paid for. For example, digital goods such as videogames or paid content within these games, music and films, etc. should remain accessible after the end of their support and should be offered for download at any time after purchase, in order to ensure that the product can be stored and continued to be used under its own responsibility. See, for example, the Stop Destroying Videogames Initiative. It is not fair to the buyer that it is possible to pay for digital goods and be deprived of access at any time! In addition, the Digital Fairness Act was intended to prevent firms, through their monopoly and systemic importance, from laying down the rules and conduct of companies through their own motivations. This is prone to political or capital-driven influence and the same, and not democratic as it does not allow society and for which the government is representative of the rules. For example, it must not be possible for payment service providers such as Visa and co to determine, according to their own moral compass (or the influence of third parties by their systemic relevance), which products may be placed on markets, otherwise they are collectively deprived of any pay method, even though the products are legal under the law, which amounts to extortion. This is contrary to the freedom of development and is neither fair to the consumer nor to the individual markets because it is de facto for the payment service provider to determine which markets and websites are available to the user rather than the government through the law. Companies are not the moral judges of the people! The same applies, for example, to censorship of social media platforms or AI providers. By censorship and TOS, such companies, whether corporate or systemically important, must not be able to determine what can be said or shown if, according to the law, it is also contrary to freedom of development and can thus determine, for their own purposes, what may be spoken and debated in a society and therefore too much influence over that company. In a democracy, the majority of the people determine the rules for society, the government is representative by law, not mega firms with potentially questionable motivations by their power and systemic importance! Adult citizens decide what they consume, what they communicate or do in their private life as long as it is legal under the law, no third party has to make judgements and restrict them!
